Package com.stevenlagoy.presidency.util

Class RandomOperations

java.lang.Object
com.stevenlagoy.presidency.util.RandomOperations
public final class RandomOperations extends Object

  • Method Details

    • randBool

      public static boolean randBool()
      Returns true or false with equal liklihood.
      Returns:
      true 50% of the time and false 50% of the time.
      See Also:
      • randChance(float)

    • flipACoin

      public static boolean flipACoin()
      Flips a coin and returns true if heads, false if tails.

    • rollDie

      public static int rollDie(int numSides)
      Rolls a die with the given number of sides and returns the result.

    • rollDice

      public static int[] rollDice(int numDice, int numSidesPer)
      Rolls the given number of dice with the given number of sides on each and returns an array with the results.

    • rollDice

      public static int[] rollDice(int[] diceSides)
      Rolls dice with the given numbers of sides and returns an array with the results.

    • rollDiceSum

      public static int rollDiceSum(int numDice, int numSidesPer)
      Rolls the given number of dice with the given number of sides and returns the sum.

    • rollDiceSum

      public static int rollDiceSum(int[] diceSides)
      Rolls dice with the given numbers of sides and returns the sum.

    • randPercent

      public static float randPercent()
      Selects a float between 0.0 and 1.0 which can be used as a percentage.
      Returns:
      A pseudorandomly selected float in the range [0.0, 1.0)
      See Also:
      • randPercent(float, float)

    • randFloat

      public static float randFloat()
      Selects a float between 0.0 and 1.0.
      Returns:
      A pseudorandomly selected float in the range [0.0, 1.0)
      See Also:
      • randFloat(float, float)

    • randPercent

      public static float randPercent(float min, float max)
      Selects a float between the min and the max.

      If min is a larger value than max, their values will be swapped.

      Parameters:
      min - The minimum value which can be selected (inclusive)
      max - The maximum value which can be selected (exclusive)
      Returns:
      A pseudorandomly selected float in the range [min, max)

    • randFloat

      public static float randFloat(float min, float max)
      Selects a float between the min and the max.

      If min is a larger value than max, their values will be swapped.

      Parameters:
      min - The minimum value which can be selected (inclusive)
      max - The maximum value which can be selected (exclusive)
      Returns:
      A pseudorandomly selected float in the range [min, max)

    • randChance

      public static boolean randChance(float chance)

    • randDouble

      public static double randDouble(double min, double max)
      Selects a double between the min and the max.
      Parameters:
      min - The minimum value which can be selected (inclusive)
      max - The maximum value which can be selected (exclusive)
      Returns:
      A pseudorandomly selected double in the range [min, max)

    • randInt

      public static int randInt(int max)
      Selects an integer between zero and the max, evenly distributed.
      Parameters:
      max - The maximum value which can be randomly selected (exclusive)
      Returns:
      A pseudorandomly generated integer in the range [0, max)

    • randInt

      public static int randInt(int min, int max)
      Selects an integer between the min and the max, evenly distributed.
      Parameters:
      min - The minimum value which can be selected (inclusive)
      max - The maximum value which can be selected (exclusive)
      Returns:
      A pseudorandomly generated integer in the range [min, max)

    • randSelect

      public static <T> T randSelect(T[] items)
      Selects one value from an array.
      Parameters:
      items - The array to select from.
      Returns:
      One randomly selected value.

    • randSelect

      public static <T> T randSelect(Collection<T> items)
      Selects one value from a collection.
      Parameters:
      items - The collection to select from.
      Returns:
      One randomly selected value.

    • weightedRandSelect

      public static <T> T weightedRandSelect(T[] items, double[] weights)
      Selects a value from the items array, with the weight for selection given by the weights array. The arrays must have the same length.
      Type Parameters:
      T - Object
      Parameters:
      items - Array of selectable values. Must have same length as weights array.
      weights - Array of weights for each value. Must have same length as items array. For any index n within the items of either array, items[n] corresponds to weights[n]. The probability that any item i will be selected is given by weights[i] / sum(weights)
      Returns:
      One value selected from the items array, or null if unsuccessful.

    • weightedRandSelect

      public static <T> T weightedRandSelect(List<T> items, List<Number> weights)
      Selects a value from the items array, with the weight for selection given by the weights array. The arrays must have the same length.
      Type Parameters:
      T - Object
      Parameters:
      items - Ordered collection of selectable values. Must have same size as weights array.
      weights - Ordered collection of weights for each value. Must have same size as items array. For any index n within the items of either collection, items[n] corresponds to weights[n]. The probability that any item i will be selected is given by weights[i] / sum(weights)
      Returns:
      One value selected from the items collection, or null if unsuccessful.

    • weightedRandSelect

      public static <T> T weightedRandSelect(Map<T,? extends Number> items)
      Selects a value from the items map, with the weight for selection given by the item's map value.
      Type Parameters:
      T - Object
      Parameters:
      items - Map of Object to Number, where the keys are Objects to select from and the values are the weights for each object. The probability that any item i will be selected is given by items[i] / sum(items.values)
      Returns:
      One value selected from the items map, or null if unsuccessful.

    • probabilisticCount

      public static int probabilisticCount(float f)
